mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, which suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and durable goods demand, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series decouples, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with a p-value greater than 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces demand destruction, generating short-term positive momentum in S&P 500 returns as markets price in sustained earnings.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and increases discount rates, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates, or forward returns turn positive in more than 60% of high-inflation and high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with rising aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal is fully subsumed by monetary policy variables in stepwise regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annually signals demand exhaustion and heightened recession probability, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with a statistically significant difference.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total payrolls indicate a widening positive output gap and robust economic activity, which drives corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85%, or forward returns are negative in more than 55% of co-expansion periods.
